liked_posts.summary: originally, there was just 1 day of christmas. then twelve. then it expanded to cover the first 24 days of december. november...
liked_posts.body:
markovchaingang:

originally, there was just 1 day of christmas. then twelve. then it expanded to cover the first 24 days of december. november was conquered in its entirety by christmas somewhere in the early 2010s. even october is showing signs of faltering. if current trends continue it’s possible that it could expand to the point where it’s christmas more often than not by the late 2030s. gentlemen; the war on christmas is not an ideological war, nor is it an economic war. it is a war for survival

liked_posts.summary: leaves excised from a copy of the handbook of canon law known simply as the decretum written by gratian, an italian camaldolese...
liked_posts.body:
theinternetarchive:

leaves excised from a copy of the handbook of canon law known simply as the decretum written by gratian, an italian camaldolese monk, around 1130-40. the decretum was widely copied and consulted throughout the middle ages. consanguinity diagrams were used to show the degree of kinship between an individual and his or her “blood” relations. These tables followed an established tradition dating back to around ad 600. (via The Cleveland Museum)

‘table of consanguinity’ and 'table of affinity,’ leafs from gratian’s decretum, italy, probably naples c. 1270-1300.

These tables followed an established tradition dating back to around ad 600. (via The Cleveland Museum)
